content: RBAC pitfalls in warehouse/procurement systems: default grant of admin access ('just in case') turns RBAC into a security liability, privilege creep where staff accumulate permissions from old roles, monolithic roles (single 'admin' covering all modules instead of narrower operational roles), and enforcing authorization only on the UI without server-side checks allowing direct API exploitation. Prevention: implement least-privilege from the start, enforce authorization at the API/service layer not just the UI, separate roles by function (warehouse staff vs procurement staff vs manager), add periodic permission audits.
